project: Stock Prediction Platform — Macro Data Ingestion & Dashboard Toggle
description: Populate the empty macro data tables (feast_yfinance_macro, feast_fred_macro, macro_fred_daily) with real data and redesign the Dashboard so users can cleanly toggle between Market View (treemap/movers/sentiment) and Macro View (FRED + yfinance indicators). Every story verified via Playwright MCP with screenshots. Goal: a production system where the Macro Environment panel shows real numbers, not all dashes.

userStories.title: Create feast_yfinance_macro table and populate it with VIX/SPY/sector ETF data
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the feast_yfinance_macro table created and populated with real yfinance macro data (VIX, SPY return, sector ETF returns, 52-week high/low) so the MacroPanel can display live values.
acceptanceCriteria: Run create_macro_table() to create feast_yfinance_macro in k8s postgres — exec into any ml or api pod that has the ml package available

Verify table created: kubectl exec -n storage deployment/postgresql -- psql -U stockuser -d stockdb -c '\d feast_yfinance_macro' returns schema with ticker, timestamp, vix, spy_return, sector_return, high52w_pct, low52w_pct columns

Populate with yfinance data via write_yfinance_macro_to_db() inside an ml pod — OR if yfinance fetch fails in k8s (no internet), inject 90 days of synthetic data directly via psql: VIX=18.5, spy_return=0.0012, sector_return=0.0008, high52w_pct=0.85, low52w_pct=0.35 for ticker='SPY'
Verify rows exist: kubectl exec -n storage deployment/postgresql -- psql -U stockuser -d stockdb -c 'SELECT COUNT(*), MIN(timestamp), MAX(timestamp) FROM feast_yfinance_macro;' returns COUNT > 0
curl http://localhost:8010/market/macro/latest — confirm vix and spy_return fields are no longer null
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: feast_yfinance_macro table existed with 90 rows of synthetic SPY data. Fixed stale Redis null cache. API now returns vix=18.5, spy_return=0.0012. Dashboard shows VIX=18.50 and SPY RETURN=+0.12%.

userStories.title: Create macro_fred_daily table and populate feast_fred_macro with FRED series data
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the macro_fred_daily table created and feast_fred_macro populated with the 14 FRED series so the MacroPanel shows real macro indicator values.
acceptanceCriteria: Create macro_fred_daily table in k8s postgres via direct DDL: columns as_of_date (date PK), dgs2, dgs10, t10y2y, t10y3m, bamlh0a0hym2, dbaa, t10yie, dcoilwtico, dtwexbgs, dexjpus, icsa, nfci, cpiaucsl, pcepilfe (all double precision), created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
Check if FRED_API_KEY is set: kubectl exec -n ingestion deployment/stock-api -- printenv FRED_API_KEY
If FRED_API_KEY exists: exec fetch_fred_macro() + write_fred_macro_to_db() to populate both tables

If FRED_API_KEY is NOT set: inject realistic representative values for last 90 days directly into feast_fred_macro AND macro_fred_daily via kubectl exec psql INSERT. Values: dgs2=4.82, dgs10=4.35, t10y2y=-0.47, t10y3m=-1.15, bamlh0a0hym2=312.0, dbaa=5.89, t10yie=2.31, dcoilwtico=71.5, dtwexbgs=108.2, dexjpus=149.8, icsa=215000, nfci=-0.18, cpiaucsl=314.5, pcepilfe=121.3
Verify feast_fred_macro COUNT > 0 and macro_fred_daily COUNT > 0
curl http://localhost:8010/market/macro/latest — ALL numeric fields must be non-null numbers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created macro_fred_daily (as_of_date PK, 15 FRED raw columns) and feast_fred_macro tables. Injected 90 days of synthetic data into both. Fixed market_service.py SQL to map raw FRED column names (bamlh0a0hym2→baml_hy_oas, dcoilwtico→wti_crude, dtwexbgs→usd_broad, pcepilfe→core_pce, ORDER BY as_of_date). Rebuilt and redeployed stock-api. All 9 MacroPanel cards now show real values (VIX=18.50, DGS10=4.35, HY Spread=312, WTI=71.50, etc.). 0 console errors.

userStories.title: Fix /market/macro/latest endpoint to read from correct tables and return real values
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the API endpoint to correctly join macro_fred_daily and feast_yfinance_macro so the MacroPanel receives real numbers.
acceptanceCriteria: Read services/api/app/services/market_service.py get_macro_latest() function in full
Verify it queries macro_fred_daily for FRED fields — if it queries feast_fred_macro or wrong table, fix the SQL to use macro_fred_daily ORDER BY as_of_date DESC LIMIT 1
Verify it queries feast_yfinance_macro for vix and spy_return — if it queries wrong table or wrong columns, fix

Ensure column name mapping is correct: macro_fred_daily.bamlh0a0hym2 → MacroLatestResponse.baml_hy_oas, macro_fred_daily.dcoilwtico → wti_crude, macro_fred_daily.dtwexbgs → usd_broad, macro_fred_daily.dgs2 → dgs2, macro_fred_daily.t10yie → t10yie

Rebuild and redeploy stock-api if Python changes made: eval $(minikube docker-env) && docker build -t stock-api:latest stock-prediction-platform/services/api/ && kubectl rollout restart deployment/stock-api -n ingestion
curl http://localhost:8010/market/macro/latest — as_of_date non-null, at least 6 numeric fields non-null
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/ via Playwright MCP — scroll to Macro Environment section
Take screenshot macro-data-live.png — at least 6 of 9 cards show real numbers
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redesign Dashboard: Market/Macro toggle replaces confusing stacked layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean toggle at the top of the Dashboard to switch between Market View (treemap, movers, sentiment) and Macro View (FRED + yfinance indicators) so the two datasets are not confusingly mixed together.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MUI ToggleButtonGroup with two options 'Market' and 'Macro' to Dashboard.tsx, positioned below the PageHeader, above all content
Market View (default, value='market'): renders existing sections — ticker strip, treemap, top gainers/losers, sentiment panel
Macro View (value='macro'): renders only MacroPanel with fullView=true prop — no treemap, no movers, no sentiment
Remove MacroPanel from the bottom of Market View entirely — it must ONLY render when Macro view is selected
Dashboard.tsx uses useState<'market' | 'macro'>('market') for the toggle
TypeScript 0 errors, npm run build succeeds
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/ via Playwright MCP
Take screenshot dashboard-market-view.png — Market tab active, treemap visible, no MacroPanel cards
Click the Macro toggle button via mcp__playwright__browser_click
Take screenshot dashboard-macro-view.png — MacroPanel cards visible with real values, no treemap
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enhance MacroPanel with expanded card layout and plain-English descriptions for Macro View
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Macro View cards to be larger and show a plain-English description of each indicator so I can quickly understand what VIX, DGS10, ICSA etc. mean without looking them up.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fullView?: boolean prop to MacroPanel component
When fullView=true: cards use minHeight 140px, value font size 1.75rem, show description text line below the value (e.g. '10-Year Treasury Yield', 'High Yield Credit Spread', 'Weekly Jobless Claims')
When fullView=false or undefined: existing compact layout unchanged (no regression)
Dashboard.tsx passes fullView={view === 'macro'} to MacroPanel
Description text for each card uses the existing MACRO_INDICATORS config description field
TypeScript 0 errors, npm run build succeeds
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/ via Playwright MCP, click Macro toggle
Take screenshot dashboard-macro-enhanced.png — cards are tall with description text visible below values
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create daily macro ingest CronJob and run final full production verification across all tabs
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need a scheduled k8s CronJob for daily macro refresh and confirmation that all 7 UI tabs work correctly with the new dashboard layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ml/pipelines/macro_ingest_pipeline.py: standalone script calling create_macro_table(), write_yfinance_macro_to_db() for last 90 days, and (if FRED_API_KEY set) fetch_fred_macro() + write_fred_macro_to_db(); gracefully skips FRED fetch if no key, logs warning instead
Create k8s/ml/cronjob-macro-ingest.yaml: schedule '0 20 * * 1-5', ml namespace, stock-ml-pipeline:latest image, runs macro_ingest_pipeline.py
Commit and push so Argo CD syncs the CronJob
Final 7-tab Playwright production pass:
Dashboard (Market view): treemap + movers visible — screenshot final-market-view.png
Dashboard (Macro view): real macro values in cards — screenshot final-macro-view.png
Forecasts: table loads with Model column — screenshot final-forecasts.png
Models: comparison table loads — screenshot final-models.png
Drift: renders without crash — screenshot final-drift.png
Analytics: at least one panel renders — screenshot final-analytics.png
Backtest: Run Backtest for AAPL returns results — screenshot final-backtest.png
Search: AAPL search returns results — screenshot final-search.png
0 console errors across all tabs
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
